snippet.title: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella on AI
snippet.description:
While skeptics cite big concerns about job displacement with this new wave of AI, Nadella argues that AI can democratize access to new skills, helping displaced workers find new jobs. Anyone can use generative AI—essentially algorithms that can create new content, including emails, white papers, and even code—as a partner at work, even if they don’t have a traditional education.

snippet.title: MICROSOFT CEO SATYA NADELLA - “Empathy is a very important business skill” | Axel Springer Award
snippet.description:
“I don’t think empathy is a soft skill. In fact, it is the hardest skill we learn, to relate to the world, to relate to the people that matter the most to us. In fact, innovation is about meeting the unmet, unarticulated needs of customers. What’s the source of that? Some would say design thinking. But design thinking is empathy. - I would say it's actually a very important buisiness skill.” Microsoft Chairman and CEO Satya Nadella made this statement during an exclusive interview with Axel Springer CEO Mathias Döpfner.

“He rewrote the code for a new era” – Satya Nadella honored with Axel Springer Award

The first AI laudatory speech ever held at an Axel Springer Award praised Microsoft Chairman and CEO Satya Nadella as “the wizard behind the digital curtain”, “the coding conjurer at the helm of Microsoft”, and “the ultimate boss in binary”, who “rewrote the code for a new era”. This year’s award ceremony center-staged the life and achievements of the visionary entrepreneur who, in not even ten years as a CEO, has increased the revenue of Microsoft tenfold.   

A multimedia show staged in the new Axel Springer building literally highlighted the different stages of Satya Nadella’s life. From his childhood days in India to his position as CEO and Chairman of Microsoft. From his first passion, Cricket, to everything he took away from his love for the game in his career. In his conversation with Axel Springer CEO Mathias Döpfner, Satya Nadella stressed that “team sports have a huge bearing on who we become as citizens and leaders”.

Surprise guest and former cricketer Zaheer Khan said: “Cricket is a symphony, and every player is a note in that composition. (…) Leadership in cricket is about being a guiding light and source of inspiration. In cricket we lead not with arrogance but with humility. In the journey of Satya Nadella, we see a reflection of these principles in the corporate world.”

Mathias Döpfner stressed that while Satya Nadella did not found Microsoft, he certainly reinvented it, and “changed the culture in no time very radically”. The conversation on stage underlined the importance of empathy for Satya Nadella: “I don’t think empathy is a soft skill. In fact, it is the hardest skill we learn, to relate to the world, to relate to the people that matter the most to us. In fact, innovation is about meeting the unmet, unarticulated needs of customers. What’s the source of that? Some would say design thinking. But design thinking is empathy.”

Volker Wissing, Federal Minister for Digital and Transport, said in his laudatory speech: “I understand everybody who is concerned about the future in the light of the current global situation. This is why evenings and events like this are all the more important. They show that there are great leaders and visionaries among us who symbolize the future, optimism, and success – and at the same time empathy and humanity. After all, there is no doubt that progress needs role models.”

The Axel Springer Award ceremony was attended by high-ranking participants from politics and business, including Mario Federico (Chairman of McDonald’s Deutschland LLC), Marianne Janik (CEO Microsoft Germany), Philipp Justus (Country Manager Google Germany GmbH & VP Central Europe), Christian Klein (CEO SAP), Alice Mascia (CEO DAZN), Verena Pausder (Entrepreneur), Konstantin Sixt (Co-CEO Sixt SE), and Hildegard Wortmann (Board Member of AUDI AG).

About the Axel Springer Award

The Axel Springer Award honors outstanding personalities who are innovative in an extraordinary way, who create and change markets and who shape culture while at the same time shouldering their social responsibility. The award recognizes accomplishments so far and encourages further steps. At the same time, the award winners should be role models for others to bring their own ideas to fruition.

Past awardees include the Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, the BioNTech founders Özlem Türeci and Uğur Şahin, Elon Musk, founder and CEO of Tesla and SpaceX, among others, the economist and author Shoshana Zuboff, Jeff Bezos, founder and CEO of Amazon and owner of the Washington Post, Sir Tim Berners-Lee, inventor of the World Wide Web, as well as Mark Zuckerberg, founder and CEO of Meta.

The format of the award ceremony is always tailored to the individual honoree. The Axel Springer Award is a prestige award without prize money. Further information about the Axel Springer Award is available at https://www.axelspringer.com/en/axel-springer-award

Satya Nadella is an Indian-American business executive and the current CEO of Microsoft Corporation, one of the world's largest technology companies. Born in Hyderabad, India, on August 19, 1967, Nadella joined Microsoft in 1992, and since then, he has held several leadership positions in the company, including Executive Vice President of the Cloud and Enterprise group and President of the Server and Tools division. Nadella became the CEO of Microsoft in February 2014, succeeding Steve Ballmer.

Under Nadella's leadership, Microsoft has undergone a significant transformation, shifting its focus from traditional software products to cloud computing and artificial intelligence. He has also been a proponent of using technology for social good, and has emphasized the importance of empathy and inclusivity in the workplace. In 2020, he published a book titled "Hit Refresh: The Quest to Rediscover Microsoft's Soul and Imagine a Better Future for Everyone," in which he reflects on his leadership journey and the role of technology in society.

In this video he beautifully summarizes "What are some effective strategies and best practices for running successful meetings that achieve their objectives, engage participants, and maximize productivity?"

snippet.title: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella Asks ChatGPT About The Best South Indian Tiffin.
snippet.description:
#chatgpt #satyanadella #microsoft #southindianfood #vadapav #biryani 
Biryani is not just a popular dish in India, but it is also an emotion. There are only a few people who don't like the delectable rice-based dish, but there are many who can eat it every day. And, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella loves it too. How do we know, you ask? Well, while speaking at the Future Ready Technology Summit in Bengaluru on Wednesday, he had a disagreement with ChatGPT over biryani after it listed the dish as a popular South Indian tiffin. It all started when Satya Nadella asked ChatGPT, a popular AI-enabled software, and a chat robot, to list the most popular South Indian tiffin items. While the list included dishes such as idli, dosa, and vada, it also had biryani. That did not go down well with Nadella, who told ChatGPT that as a Hyderabadi, the software cannot insult his intelligence by calling biryani a South Indian tiffin.

snippet.title: Microsoft's Satya Nadella: Why we need re-founders | Masters of Scale
snippet.description:
To achieve massive scale, you don’t just need founders, you also need a re-founder – someone to come in at a later stage to keep the mission and culture on track. As Microsoft’s third CEO ever – after Bill Gates and Steve Ballmer – Satya Nadella is doing just that. He discusses how he has transformed Microsoft from a cutthroat culture towards embracing social networks, collaboration, and cloud.

Follow the Masters of Scale podcast: https://listen.mastersofscale.com/follow
Subscribe to the Masters of Scale Newsletter: https://hubs.la/Q01RPQH-0

On Masters of Scale, iconic business leaders share lessons and strategies that have helped them grow the world's most fascinating companies. Founders, CEOs, and dynamic innovators (from companies like Uber, Airbnb, Apple, and Disney) join candid conversations about their triumphs and challenges with a set of luminary hosts, including founding host Reid Hoffman — LinkedIn co-founder and Greylock partner. From navigating early prototypes to expanding brands globally, Masters of Scale provides priceless insights to help anyone grow their dream enterprise.

snippet.title: “Don't wait for your next job to do your best work.” Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella | #LinkedIn #Shorts
snippet.description:
Don’t wait for a promotion? Do this instead. 

*Think of your job as your manager’s job.* 

This mental reframe helped @Microsoft — parent company of LinkedIn — CEO Satya Nadella prepare himself for the next level without waiting for a promotion. “You cannot grow unless you think your growth comes from what you’re doing,” he says. 

Watch the full interview here: https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/dont-wait-your-next-job-do-best-work-microsoft-ceo-satya-roslansky/

snippet.title: The Power Of Empathy Satya Nadella
snippet.description:
#empathy   #motivation   #life   #karma   #CNBCTV   #understanding   #leadershipskills   #entrepreneur   #measuresuccess   #reachgoals 

Welcome to our channel, where we explore the fascinating world of innovation and its driving force - empathy, as we are joined by some of the most prominent figures in the tech industry, including Microsoft's CEO. In this thought-provoking video, we delve into the essence of design thinking, uncovering how empathy plays a crucial role in successfully meeting unmet and unarticulated needs of customers.

As we explore the transformative potential of empathy, we'll also touch upon the role of AI, artificial intelligence, and robots in shaping the future of tech and innovation. Understanding the intersection of technology and human emotions is vital in creating solutions that truly resonate with people's lives.

Innovation has always been about understanding the pain points, desires, and aspirations of our customers. But how do we tap into those hidden needs that often lie beneath the surface? The answer lies in our ability to listen between the lines and empathize deeply with the people we aim to serve.

Throughout the video, we'll also discuss the leadership skills required to foster an empathetic culture within organizations. Leading with empathy is crucial in driving innovation and cultivating an environment where creativity flourishes.

Drawing upon life's experiences and real-world examples from the tech industry, we explore how empathy is a skill that can be nurtured, learned, and refined over time. Our journey to success, both in personal and professional realms, is shaped by our capacity to connect with others on a profound level.

Design thinking, as we'll discover, is not merely a process but a mindset grounded in empathy. We'll explore how leading organizations, including Microsoft under its visionary CEO, have embraced empathy to revolutionize products and services, leaving a lasting impact on their customers.

Join us as we delve into the depths of empathy and learn how this profound understanding of human emotions and needs can unlock the door to innovation. By watching this video, you'll gain valuable insights into how empathy becomes the wellspring of creativity, pushing us to think beyond the obvious and reimagine solutions to the most complex challenges.

Whether you're an aspiring entrepreneur, a seasoned innovator, or simply curious about the power of empathy in shaping a better world, this video is a must-watch. Together, let's explore the transformative potential of empathy and harness it to drive meaningful change in our lives and society.

Don't forget to hit the like button if you enjoyed the content, and subscribe to our channel for more engaging discussions on innovation, design thinking, and the boundless possibilities of empathy, as we continue to feature prominent figures in the tech industry and other visionary leaders.

Turn on the notification bell so you won't miss any of our upcoming videos that will inspire you to embrace empathy in your quest for innovation and success. Thank you for joining us on this enlightening journey of understanding the true source of success in any innovative agenda - the incredible power of empathy. Let's shape a world where innovation thrives, guided by the deep connection we establish with the unmet and unarticulated needs of our fellow human beings.
